steps.title: Read the leg lengths from the diagram
steps.content: The right triangle has legs of length 3 (vertical) and 7 (horizontal), with a right angle marked at the corner.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle with legs $a$ and $b$ and hypotenuse $c$:
$$c^2 = 3^2 + 7^2 = 9 + 49 = 58$$
$$c = \sqrt{58}$$

steps.title: Evaluate the square root
steps.content: Since $7^2 = 49$ and $8^2 = 64$, we know $\sqrt{58}$ falls between 7 and 8. Testing more precisely: $7.6^2 = 57.76$ and $7.62^2 \approx 58.06$, so $\sqrt{58} \approx 7.616$. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
